Amgen Lung Cancer Drug Gets Full Approval From FDA
data.description:
By Terell Wright


Amgen's Imdelltra received full approval from the Food and Drug Administration for use in patients with lung cancer.

The biotechnology company said Wednesday Imdelltra received approval for adult patients with extensive stage small cell lung cancer with disease progression on or after platinum-based chemotherapy.

The approval comes after a study showed Imdelltra reduced the risk of death by 40% and extended median overall survival by more than five months, compared to the existing standard of care chemotherapy treatment.

"The FDA's decision reinforces Imdelltra as a recognized standard of care for people living with extensive stage small cell lung cancer whose disease progressed on or after frontline therapy," said Jay Bradner, Amgen's executive vice president of research and development.


Write to Terell Wright at terell.wright@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

11-19-25 1655ET

Yann LeCun to leave Meta, launch AI startup focused on Advanced Machine Intelligence
data.description:
(Reuters) -Yann LeCun, one of the founding figures of modern artificial intelligence and a pivotal force at Meta Platforms, said on Wednesday he plans to leave the company at the end of the year to launch a new AI startup.

LeCun has been a key part of Meta's artificial intelligence ambitions for more than a decade. He joined the company in 2013 to create Facebook AI Research (FAIR), the in-house lab that helped transform Meta into one of the AI leaders.

Over 12 years, he served five as FAIR's founding director and seven as the company's chief AI scientist, guiding breakthroughs in deep learning, computer vision and large-scale language modeling that underpin products like Instagram recommendations and Meta's generative AI systems.

He developed an early form of an artificial neural network that mimicked how the human eye and brain process images -- technology that later became the backbone of modern image recognition and GenAI.

LeCun, 65, said his new venture will pursue Advanced Machine Intelligence (AMI) research -- a project he has developed in collaboration with colleagues at FAIR and New York University, where he teaches.

The computer scientist said he will provide more details on his new firm at a later date, but added that Meta will be a partner in the venture, reflecting what he called the company's "continued interest and support" for AMI's long-term goals.

"The creation of FAIR is my proudest non-technical accomplishment," he wrote. "The impact of FAIR on Meta, the AI field, and the wider world has been spectacular."

Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg and CTO Andrew Bosworth have both credited LeCun with laying the foundations for Meta's current AI infrastructure, including its open-source Llama models that have become a cornerstone of the global AI research community.

LeCun is widely regarded as one of the "godfathers" of deep learning, alongside Geoffrey Hinton and Yoshua Bengio -- a trio that won the 2018 Turing Award, often called the Nobel Prize of computing.

(Reporting by Akash Sriram in Bengaluru; Editing by Alan Barona)

Kakao Healthcare Co., Ltd. announced that it expects to receive KRW 40 billion in funding from Kakao Corp.
data.description:
Kakao Healthcare Co., Ltd. announced private placement of 8,487,163 common shares at an issue price of KRW 4,713.000092 per share for the gross proceeds of KRW 40,000,000,000 on November 19, 2025. The transaction will include participation from returning investor, Kakao Corp. The transaction has been approved by the board of directors of the company.

The transaction is expected to close on first half of 2026.

data.title: Regeneron Gets FDA Approval for Eye Treatment Eylea
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton


Regeneron Pharmaceuticals received approval from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration for its eye treatment Eylea HD.

The approval is for Eylea to treat macular edema following retinal vein occlusion with up to every eight-week dosing after an initial dosing period. The FDA also approved a monthly dosing option for some patients, the Tarrytown, N.Y., biopharmaceutical company said Wednesday.

The approval is based on trial data showing patients treated with Eylea achieved visual acuity gains.

Regeneron is still working with Novo Nordisk's subsidiary Catalent Indiana to develop a pre-filled syringe version of Eylea. It is trying to resolve issues identified from a general site inspection by the FDA in July.


Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

11-19-25 1648ET

Palo Alto Networks to Buy Chronosphere for $3.35 Billion
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall


Palo Alto Networks is buying the observability platform Chronosphere for $3.35 billion.

Cybersecurity company Palo Alto said the cash-and-stock deal would address demands for observability in the rapidly expanding artificial-intelligence data-center market, combining Chronosphere's observability architecture with Palo Alto's AI-powered AgentiX tool.

"Chronosphere was built to scale for the data demands of the AI era from day one, which is why it is chosen by leading AI-native and born-in-the-cloud organizations," Palo Alto Networks Chief Executive Nikesh Arora said. "And once we leverage AgentiX with Chronosphere, we will take observability from simple dashboards to real-time, agentic remediation. We are excited to not just enter this space, but to disrupt it."

The deal is expected to close in the second half of Palo Alto's fiscal-year 2026.


Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

11-19-25 1643ET

Lowe's Profits Top Estimates as TJX Raises Outlook
data.description:
Lowe's Cos. (LOW) reported profit that topped expectations on a pickup in online sales and growth in demand from professional contractors. The results mark a minor bright spot in retailer results this week after competitor Home Depot (HD) cut its full-year profit target on Tuesday as consumers pulled back on bigger ticket purchases. Elsewhere, TJX Cos., which runs chains including TJ Maxx and Marshalls, posted sales above estimates last quarter, raising its outlook, and perhaps signaling that US shoppers are turning to cheaper options as the economy shows signs of stress. Bloomberg's Redd Brown and Lily Meier joined Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec on 'Bloomberg Businessweek Daily' to break it all down.

Bloomberg videos, provided by MT Newswires
